FROM STORM SURVEY: TORNADO MOVED SOUTHWARD OUT OF NEBRASKA AND DESTROYED OUTBUILDINGS AND SNAPPED TREES ALONG STATE LINE ROAD. THE TORNADO TRAVELED 2 MILES TO THE SSE, AND LIFTED ALONG US HIGHWAY 77, 2.5 MILES WNW OF OKETO. THE TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 50 YARDS WIDE.

DAMAGE SURVEY: TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3 MILES SOUTH OF OKETO AND TRAVELED SSW FOR 4.5 MILES DAMAGING TREES AND SNAPPING POWER POLES. A BARN AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED 3 MILES NE OF MARYSVILLE WHERE THE TORNADO LIFTED.
REPORTS OF TORNADO ON THE GROUND.
RAIN WRAPPED TORNADO NEAR HIGHWAY 77.
DEBRIS ALONG NORTH STREET IN MARYSVILLE. MANY TREES DOWN ALONG NORTH STREET.

STORM SURVEY: A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3 NNE OF BLUE RAPIDS AND TRAVELED 1.6 MILES SSW AND LIFTED 1.4 MILES NNE OF BLUE RAPIDS. THE TORNADO BLEW OVER RAIL CARS JUST WEST OF US 77 2.2 MILES NNE OF BLUE RAPIDS, KS. TORNADO WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.

DELAYED REPORT: SCATTERED DAMAGE ACROSS MINNEAPOLIS INCLUDING SNAPPED POWER POLES, A ROOF TORN OFF A CHURCH, LARGE TREES UPROOTED AND SNAPPED, AND WINDOW SCREENS BLOWN OFF THE COURTHOUSE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

WIDESPREAD DAMAGE OCCURRED EARLY THURSDAY MORNING ACROSS MINNEAPOLIS INCLUDING: LARGE TREES UPROOTED, TREE TRUNKS SNAPPED, SNAPPED POWER POLES, A ROOF TORN OFF A CHURCH, AND WINDOW SCREENS BLOWN OFF THE COURTHOUSE. AFTER ANALYSIS OF RADAR DATA, FIRST-HAND ACCOUNTS AND PICTURES OF THE DAMAGE, WE CONCLUDE THAT STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS OF 95 MPH PRODUCED THIS DAMAGE.
